reasoning: The resolution is based on the 'Vaccine(s)' column for 'Hepatitis C' in the Wikipedia List of infectious diseases. As of the cutoff date (Dec 21, 2025), there is no approved Hepatitis C vaccine, and major clinical trials are still in Phase 1 or early stages (e.g., NIH RFA-AI-25-012 for FY 2026, 'Rational Design of Vaccines Against Hepatitis C Virus'). The timeline for a new vaccine to go from these early stages to full approval and then addition to the specific Wikipedia article is on the order of many years. Even if there were a sudden breakthrough, the likelihood of a fully approved vaccine being added to Wikipedia within the 1-3 month resolution horizon is practically zero. However, giving a non-zero minimum bound due to the possibility of Wikipedia vandalism or highly uncharacteristic rapid Wikipedia edits, I estimate the probability is effectively the minimum allowed value (0.02) for all three resolution dates. There is no information suggesting an imminent approval that would change this.
